name: Mashed Potato Ghosts
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks (about 5 cups)
2 tablespoons freshly chopped parsley or dill (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place potatoes in a large pot and add enough water to cover. Bring to boil.
2. Cook 13 to 15 minutes, or until very tender. Drain in a colander.
3. Return cooked potatoes to the pot and stir over medium heat about 1 minute,
until excess water has evaporated.
4. Remove from heat and, using a potato masher, mash into hot milk, butter,
salt, and pepper. Beat with a wooden spoon until potatoes are smooth
and creamy. Stir in optional add-ins if desired.
5. Create stencils of ghost shapes out of heavy-duty aluminum foil.
6. Place stencils on a cookie sheet or microwaveable serving dish.
7. Working with 1/2 to 1 cup mashed potatoes per ghost, use a rubber spatula
to fill in stencil's ghost shapes. Lift stencils off finished ghosts.
8. Using a small paring knife, slice olives to create circular shapes for eyes and mouth.
9. To warm ghosts, microwave on high (100% power) 3 to 5 minutes on a microwavable dish.
If using an oven, place potatoes on a cookie sheet and reheat at 350
degrees, loosely covered with foil, 10 to 15 minutes or until heated through.
10. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
